epic: Wave 3 — Vision Upgrade + Blog Pipeline + Test Coverage

stories.title: Upgrade vision model to llama-3.2-11b-vision-instruct
stories.description: Upgrade from @cf/llava-hf/llava-1.5-7b-hf (7B, hallucination-prone) to @cf/meta/llama-3.2-11b-vision-instruct (11B). Changes: (1) Update VISION_MODEL in src/worker/lib/constants.ts, (2) Update AI.run() calls in both upload.ts and openapi-check-image.ts — new model uses messages array format: AI.run(model, { messages: [{ role: 'user', content: [{ type: 'image', image: arrayOfBytes }, { type: 'text', text: prompt }] }], max_tokens: 512 }), (3) Update response parsing — new model returns { response: string }. Read the Cloudflare Workers AI docs for @cf/meta/llama-3.2-11b-vision-instruct to confirm the exact API format before making changes. Run npx tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run to verify.
acceptance_criteria: Both upload.ts and openapi-check-image.ts use new API message format
Response parsing handles new model output format

stories.title: Add vision output validation + confidence calibration
stories.description: Add validation layer for vision model responses to catch hallucinations. Create src/worker/lib/vision-validator.ts with: (1) validateVisionResponse(text: string): boolean — reject responses too short (<20 chars), containing no Romanian text, or incoherent gibberish, (2) calibrateConfidence(response: string, verdict: string): number — score confidence based on response quality (keyword density, coherence signals) instead of fixed values (currently 0.85/0.60/0.80). Use validator in both upload.ts and openapi-check-image.ts. When validation fails, fall back to 'suspicious' with confidence 0.0. Add unit tests in src/worker/lib/vision-validator.test.ts. Run npx tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run to verify.
acceptance_criteria: Both upload.ts and openapi-check-image.ts use the validator
Incoherent responses produce low-confidence suspicious verdict
Unit tests cover valid, invalid, and edge-case responses

stories.title: Add Opus-benchmarked image analysis tests
stories.description: Add tests that benchmark the CF vision model against Claude Opus as ground truth. Steps: (1) Create test-fixtures/images/ with 3 test images — download a known phishing screenshot URL, a known safe screenshot URL, and an ambiguous one (use placeholder PNG files with embedded text if real screenshots aren't available), (2) Create test-fixtures/opus-baselines.json with pre-computed Opus verdicts for each image (manually populate with expected verdicts: phishing, likely_safe, suspicious), (3) Create src/worker/routes/vision-quality.test.ts — for each fixture image, mock the CF AI.run() with realistic responses matching each verdict, call the image check handler, verify the endpoint verdict doesn't contradict the Opus baseline (e.g. Opus=phishing means endpoint must not return likely_safe), (4) Test the fallback path — when AI.run() throws, verify confidence is 0.0 not a fake high value. Run npx tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run to verify.
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify CF model verdict doesn't contradict Opus baseline
Tests cover AI.run() failure path with confidence 0.0

stories.title: Add missing secret detection in Sanity publish + cron logging
stories.description: Fix the blog pipeline that silently fails because SANITY_WRITE_TOKEN is missing. Changes: (1) In src/worker/lib/sanity.ts — before any Sanity mutation, check if SANITY_WRITE_TOKEN (from env) is present. If missing, log structuredLog('error', 'sanity_write_token_missing', { action: 'publish' }) and return early with a meaningful error, don't throw. (2) In src/worker/lib/cron-handler.ts — add structuredLog at start/end of each cron job with job name and duration. In runSanityPublish, check for SANITY_WRITE_TOKEN and log clear error if missing. Ensure one failing job doesn't block others. (3) Add unit tests for the missing-secret paths in cron-handler.test.ts — mock env without SANITY_WRITE_TOKEN and verify the handler logs error but doesn't throw. Read both files first to understand existing patterns. Run npx tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run to verify.
acceptance_criteria: Sanity publish logs clear error when SANITY_WRITE_TOKEN is missing
Cron handler logs job name and duration for each job
One failing cron job does not prevent others from running

stories.title: Upgrade Bowser story assertions to structural checks
stories.description: In the existing Bowser/browser detection tests, upgrade weak assertions from body.contains() to proper structural checks. Read the existing test files first (search for bowser or browser-related test files under src/worker/ or e2e/). Replace string contains checks with structured assertions: verify JSON shape, check specific fields, assert types. Run npx tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run to verify.
acceptance_criteria: All Bowser-related assertions use structural checks (toBe, toEqual, toHaveProperty) not string contains

stories.title: Add asset integrity tests — favicon, logo, brand colors
stories.description: Create e2e/asset-integrity.spec.ts (Playwright test). Test that critical static assets load correctly: (1) GET /favicon.ico returns valid icon (200 or 204, content-type image/*), (2) Check that the homepage loads the site logo (query selector for img with logo in alt/src), (3) Verify brand color CSS variables are defined (check computed styles for primary brand colors). The BASE_URL is https://pre.ai-grija.ro (read from process.env.BASE_URL or default). Run npx playwright test e2e/asset-integrity.spec.ts to verify.
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify favicon loads with correct content-type
Tests verify logo image is present

stories.title: Add blog category route tests
stories.description: Create src/worker/routes/blog.test.ts with unit tests for the blog category routes. Read src/worker/routes/blog.ts (or wherever blog routes are defined) first. Test: (1) GET /blog returns valid HTML or JSON listing, (2) GET /blog/:category returns filtered posts, (3) Invalid category returns 404, (4) Blog route handles empty Sanity response gracefully. Mock the Sanity fetch calls. Run npx tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run to verify.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover category filtering and error handling

stories.title: Add tests for OpenAPI chanfana route endpoints
stories.description: Create tests for the OpenAPI/chanfana route endpoints. Read the chanfana route files under src/worker/routes/openapi-*.ts first. Create src/worker/routes/openapi-endpoints.test.ts. Test each OpenAPI endpoint: (1) CheckTextEndpoint — valid text input returns classification, (2) CheckImageEndpoint — valid image returns analysis, (3) ShareEndpoint — valid hash returns share card, (4) Test validation errors (missing required fields), (5) Test rate limiting response format. Mock env bindings (AI, CACHE, STORAGE). Run npx tsc --noEmit && npx vitest run to verify.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover all OpenAPI chanfana endpoints
Tests verify validation and error responses
